An alluvial plain is a flat, low-lying landform that is composed of sediment deposited by rivers or streams. These sediments, which can include sand, gravel, and silt, are carried by the water and deposited in a flat area, often near the mouth of the river. Alluvial plains can be found in many parts of the world and are often rich in resources, such as fertile soil for agriculture or minerals for mining.\n\nOne famous alluvial plain is the Nile Delta in Egypt, which is located at the mouth of the Nile River and is known for its fertile soil and abundant agricultural production. Other well-known alluvial plains include the Ganges-Brahmaputra Delta in South Asia, the Amazon Basin in South America, and the Mississippi Delta in the United States.

Atoms are very small! They are so small that it is difficult to imagine just how small they are. To give you an idea of how small atoms are, let's compare them to things that you might be more familiar with. A grain of sand is made up of many, many atoms. In fact, a grain of sand contains billions and billions of atoms. But even a grain of sand is relatively large compared to an atom. If we were to stretch out the atoms in a grain of sand so that they were lined up next to each other, the line of atoms would be so small that it would be invisible to the naked eye. To get a better sense of just how small atoms are, we can compare them to even smaller things, like a virus. A virus is a very small particle that can cause illness. Even a virus is much larger than an atom. If we were to compare an atom to a virus, it would be like comparing a tiny dot to a small balloon. The dot would be the atom and the balloon would be the virus. This gives you an idea of just how small atoms are. I hope this helps you understand just how small atoms are. Cross-validation is a resampling procedure used to evaluate the performance of machine learning models. It involves dividing a dataset into a training set, which is used to train the model, and a test set, which is used to evaluate the model. The idea behind cross-validation is to use a portion of the data for training and a different portion for testing, and to repeat this process multiple times in order to get a better estimate of the model's performance.



There are several different types of cross-validation, including:



K-fold cross-validation: In k-fold cross-validation, the dataset is divided into k equal-sized folds. The model is trained on k-1 folds, and the remaining fold is used as the test set. This process is repeated k times, with a different fold being used as the test set each time. The final performance measure is the average of the performance measures obtained on each of the k folds.



Leave-one-out cross-validation: In leave-one-out cross-validation, the model is trained on all but one of the data points, and the remaining point is used as the test set. This process is repeated for each data point, resulting in k test sets (where k is the number of data points in the dataset). The final performance measure is the average of the performance measures obtained on each of the k test sets.



Stratified k-fold cross-validation: In stratified k-fold cross-validation, the data is divided into k folds, but the folds are stratified to ensure that each fold has a representative proportion of each class. This is particularly useful when the classes in the dataset are imbalanced (i.e., there are significantly more examples of one class than the other).



Cross-validation is a widely used technique for evaluating the performance of machine learning models because it helps to reduce the risk of overfitting (i.e., the model performing well on the training data but poorly on unseen data). By training the model on different subsets of the data and evaluating its performance on different test sets, we can get a more robust estimate of the model's generalization ability.

One of the reasons people are so upset with Banks is this thing called the " Mortgage crisis . " Basically , in the past , houses were a very safe investment . People would borrow money from banks to buy houses and it was very safe because it was very rare that someone would not pay the bank back . It made a lot of money because they could charge a fairly low interest rate , but the loan would exist for upwards of 30 years . Then , someone came up with the idea of selling mortgages . So say you have $ 10 and I want to buy lunch . I borrow the $ 10 from you and promise that I will pay you $ 2 for every day that I do n't pay you back . So you know that I wo n't pay you back for two days , so you will make $ 4 from me . Instead of holding onto this loan , you tell Fred that I owe you $ 10 . So you tell Fred that if he gives you $ 12 , then you would sell him my debt . He then takes my debt and I owe HIM $ 14 after the end of the two days . So he makes $ 2 and you make $ 2 on the deal and I get lunch . Now you are making enough money doing this , so you do this a lot . You start lending out money to a lot of people , then bundling these debts together and selling them to other people . This is fine , because people are still paying off the debts so everyone is making money ( except the people paying , but they get a house ) . Because this is so profitable , you start lending out money to people who have no job or other source of income . People who have no chance to pay you back . You then sell that debt to Fred . You make your $ 2 , but I ca n't pay Fred , so Fred now loses $ 12 and I lose my " lunch " ( Ok , the analogy started to break down here ) . The banks loaned out money that people had no chance to repay , then went on Wall Street and started selling these mortgages for a lot of money claiming that they were " safe " bets . The people lost their houses , because they could n't pay their mortgages and the people who bought the mortgages lost their money , but the banks still made their money . As a footnote , the people who took out the mortgages should n't have taken the mortgages they could n't pay back and the people buying the mortgages from the banks should have done better research into what they were buying and the regulating bodies should have seen what was going on and raised red flags earlier . There is a lot of blame to go around , but the banks are the ones who did n't lose as much ( and were bailed out for what they did lose ) . That 's one of the reasons people are so mad at the banks specifically . Then there 's a lot of talk of inequality in pay and taxes . This is answered well by DerBafog 's response . The % daily value (DV) listed on the label of a supplement or fortified food product is a guideline that tells you how much of a particular nutrient the product provides in relation to the recommended daily amount. The daily value is based on the recommended daily intake of specific nutrients for a healthy adult diet, as determined by the US Food and Drug Administration (FDA).

For example, if a supplement label says it provides 100% of the daily value of vitamin C, it means it provides the same amount of vitamin C as what is recommended for an adult to consume in a day. If it says it provides 200% of the daily value, it means it provides twice the recommended daily amount of vitamin C.

It's important to note that the daily value is just a guideline and is not meant to be a strict requirement for everyone. Different people have different nutritional needs based on their age, sex, weight, and activity level. Some people may need more or less of certain nutrients than what is listed as the daily value.

It's also worth noting that our bodies can only absorb and use a certain amount of nutrients at a time. If we consume more than our bodies can use, the excess can be excreted through urine or feces. This is why taking very high amounts of certain nutrients can be unnecessary and potentially harmful. It's always a good idea to talk to a healthcare provider or a registered dietitian before starting a new supplement regimen.

This is an example of the system of checks and balances in our government, which helps to keep any one branch from becoming too powerful.

A pyramid scheme is a type of investment where each person involved recruits others to join. Money made by the new members funnels up to the higher members. The scheme relies on an ever-increasing number of recruits to bring in funds, but eventually, there aren't enough new members to support the upper tiers, and the whole pyramid collapses.

In a pyramid scheme, the people at the top make money, while the people at the bottom lose money. This is because there are more people at the bottom than at the top, and as the pyramid gets bigger, it becomes harder and harder for the people at the bottom to make any money.

Pyramid schemes are illegal in many countries because they are fraudulent and rely on constantly recruiting new members to bring in funds, rather than any real investment or sale of products or services. They are often disguised as legitimate investments or business opportunities, but they are not.

The National Security Agency (NSA) is a government agency responsible for collecting and analyzing communication and other electronic data for the purpose of national security. While it is important for the government to have the ability to gather information in order to protect the country and its citizens, there are concerns about the privacy of individuals when the government collects and stores their personal information.

Here are a few reasons why NSA spying on you might be a bad thing:

Invasion of privacy: When the NSA collects and stores your personal information, it is essentially invading your privacy. This can feel like a violation of your personal space and autonomy.

Lack of transparency: The NSA's activities are often shrouded in secrecy, which can make it difficult for individuals to understand exactly what the agency is doing and how it is using their personal information.

Potential for abuse: While the NSA is supposed to use its powers only for national security purposes, there have been instances in the past where government agencies have abused their powers and used them for other purposes. This can erode trust in the government and create a sense of fear and mistrust among the general population.

Legal issues: There are also legal issues surrounding the NSA's activities, including questions about the extent to which the agency is allowed to collect and use personal information. Some people believe that the NSA's activities may be unconstitutional, as they may infringe on the privacy rights of individuals.

In summary, NSA spying on you can be a bad thing because it can invade your privacy, be secretive, have the potential for abuse, and raise legal issues.

Your electric toothbrush charges through a process called induction. It works like this:

Inside the charger, there is a coil of wire. When electricity flows through the wire, it creates a magnetic field around the charger. The toothbrush has a second coil of wire inside of it. When you put the toothbrush on the charger, the magnetic field from the charger passes through the plastic and into the toothbrush. This causes electricity to flow through the toothbrush's coil of wire. The electricity then travels through the toothbrush and into the battery, charging it up.

The reason the charger base gets warm when you take the toothbrush off is because some of the energy from the charger is converted into heat. This is a normal part of the charging process.

Once upon a time , when dinosaurs and tie - dyed T - Shirts roamed the earth , there was UNIX , and it was good . Back in those days , a computer was as big as a house , and they almost all used UNIX or one of its close brothers . But then , some cheeky monkeys up near Seattle and over near Cupertino decided to start making computers a lot smaller . One of these companies was IBM . The other one was Apple . Both Apple and IBM wanted something to use on their little computers , so they found something that looked like UNIX , and acted like UNIX , but was n't really UNIX at all . The IBM company bought a little program by the name of MS - DOS . At its very core , MS - DOS was n't very special . It was ~~pretty much UNIX with a different name~~ * designed to be a simple relative to UNIX , more like a pet dog than a brother * . But it was still just a little baby . In many years , it would grow up to be a big , scary monster called Windows . But that was many years away . While IBM and MS - DOS were gobbling up the market share , a crazy old bearded fellow named Richard Stallman had a great idea . " Everything should be free ! " he said , as he watched another episode of Star Trek . " Forcing people to ~~pay for software and hiding it behind licenses and patents is evil~~ * use software that they can not change to suit their needs is evil * ! Everything should be free ! " Richard said to anyone who would listen . And his idea took hold . You see , big old UNIX and little baby MS - DOS were both systems that you had to pay for , whether you bought UNIX from Bell Labs , or MS - DOS from IBM and Microsoft , you were paying for them * and could n't change them * . But back in those days , the only kind of people who could use computers were the kind of people who could build computers and talk to their bits and bytes . So a lot of people agreed with crazy Mister Stallman , and something called the " Free Software Foundation " was formed , aiming to build a brother to UNIX and MS - DOS that was called GNU . Now , [ the gnu ] ( URL_0 ) ( pronounced guh - knew ) is a big fuzzy animal , but it 's also a name and it really means " GNU is Not Unix " . But it really was , just with another name . The whole point of GNU was to be a brother to UNIX and MS - DOS that * everyone * could add to , or change , or make work however they wanted . That 's what we mean by " Free Software " : to make it work however you want , without a big mean lawyer stomping on your face . Now , this is still many years ago . Before you were born . But a few years later , as more and more very smart people started to get their hands on more and more smaller computers , more and more of them stopped wanting to ~~pay~~ * settle * for MS - DOS . By this time , these computers were all called x86 computers , named after the tiny little brain inside . They were different from the big computers that UNIX was used for . At this time , when you bought a computer , there was just about one operating system you could put on it . If you bought a little x86 computer , you could only put on MS - DOS and its little dog Windows . If you bought a big old mainframe , you could only put on UNIX . But then a sharp young Finnish man named Linus Torvalds started to play around with making UNIX work on an x86 computer . The GNU already had all the little parts of the operating system ready : the arms and hands and ears and eyes of it were working just fine . But the GNU needed a heart . So Linus Torvalds built a heart from scratch . It was partly UNIX , and partly MS - DOS , and partly pulled out of thin air . It worked like magic on the x86 computers , and brought Free Software to the little computers . Now , twenty years have passed since then , and a lot has changed on the surface , but the heart stays the same . The big scary monster that we call Windows 7 * is the son of MS - DOS ( which was UNIX 's little dog ) and Windows NT ( which was UNIX 's second cousin . ) * And the GNU / Linux system that we know today is built on that same history : it tries to say it 's not UNIX , but deep down it really is * trying to be as much like UNIX as it can * . The only difference at the very core is that GNU / Linux is Free to use , share and change , but Windows 7 costs money ~~to~~ * and you ca n't really * do anything * to change it , or make it work better for you * . * * TL;DR * * : Windows and Linux are essentially the same , and steal from each other all the time , but at their very core , they 're mostly still operating on basic technologies designed in the 70s . Similarly , most UNIXes ( Linux , BSD , Solaris , AIX ) are the same at the core , because they use the same tools and accomplish the same tasks . This is a gross simplification . But you said to explain like you 're five , and anything more technical defeats the purpose . * * Edits * * : I ~~took some stuff away~~ , and * added some things in * to account for my flagrant lies and misrepresentation .

Water doesn't have a smell because it's made up of just hydrogen and oxygen atoms, and these atoms don't have a smell. When water is mixed with other things that have a smell, like salt or plants, then the water can pick up the smell of those things. That's why the ocean has a smell, because it contains salt and other substances. Lakes and tap water can also pick up smells from the things that are dissolved in them or from the pipes that they travel through. But pure water, which doesn't have any other substances mixed in with it, doesn't have a smell.

Men and women attempt suicide about equally . The difference in success rate is due to the methods used . Women tend to favour methods that are less certain and more likely to leave an intact body ( pills , cutting wrists . ) There is some thought that this might be due to how women are socialized ( wanting to create the least amount of mess ) . Men on the other hand succeed more because they use more violent methods . ( such as guns or jumping in front of a train ) . Again , here it is thought that socialisation plays a role . Men not only on average have more access to guns / have an interest in guns that is more encouraged , but men also tend to have more of a ' go big or go home ' kind of attitude . Sadly in this case that attitude leads to them dying more . What lays underneath this all is a society where mental health is still too stigmatised and often unaffordable for many people . People are not taught to mind their mental health as they should , and often friends and family members are not aware of the warning signs ( if any are present ) so they find it hard to sound the alarms when things start going south .

Because it works well enough . Evolution is not about making something perfect or sensible . It just happened that any little changes to the reproductive system did n't result in any benefit ( or were actually worse ) so it did n't change much over time . Though that is an understatement since reproductive organs are * vastly * different between species even if some basic similarities are in place . And anyway there is some benefit to it . Having one opening , for example the way male penises are used to excrete urine as well as to ejaculate seen , means there 's less opportunity for pathogens to enter the body . And even with two openings such as the way the female anatomy is set up , they are in close enough proximity that again it limits access to pathogens , compared to having the openings be in completely different areas of the body .

Sure! A game engine is a piece of software that helps people create video games. It includes a lot of different tools and features that make it easier for people to build games, especially if they don't want to start from scratch. One important part of a game engine is the graphics engine. This is the part of the engine that is responsible for creating the images you see on the screen while you're playing the game. It does this by using math and computer code to create 3D models of objects and characters, and then rendering them on the screen so they look like they're in a real place. The graphics engine also handles things like lighting and shading, so that the game looks realistic and believable. It also takes care of things like camera angles, so you can see different parts of the game world from different perspectives. Overall, the game engine is a really important tool for game developers because it helps them build and create their games more quickly and easily.

Eye twitching, also called blepharospasm, is when your eyelid muscle involuntarily spasms or twitches. It is usually painless and harmless, and it usually goes away on its own within a few days. There are many possible causes of eye twitching, such as fatigue, stress, dry eyes, and certain medications. It can also be caused by certain medical conditions, such as blepharitis (inflammation of the eyelids) and Tourette syndrome (a disorder that causes repeated twitching and other movements).

To help prevent eye twitching, you can try to get enough sleep, drink plenty of water, and try to reduce stress in your life. If your eye twitching is severe or doesn't go away after a few days, you should talk to your doctor. They can help you figure out what might be causing the twitching and suggest ways to help stop it.

This is an example of something called market segmentation . Retailers want to charge as much as possible for things , so ideally they will charge each person as much as that person is willing to pay . Take washing powder . If upper middle class people are prepared to pay £ 5 for a kilo of powder , the supermarket wants to charge that much . But they also do n't want to lose sales from the people who are only willing to pay £ 1 . So , they sell branded washing powder in a nice looking box for £ 5 , and own - brand powder in a plain box for £ 1 . The rich people believe they are getting superior quality , and the poor people believe they are getting a bargain . In fact , it is often the case that the same company makes both powders , they may even be identical . If you want to know for sure , compare the ingredients list . Often store brand products contain the same ingredients as the branded products . Sometimes there is an actual difference between the two products , but the profit margin on the expensive one is still usually much higher than the cheap one . There may be an actual difference in quality , but what matters is that there is a perceived difference . Purchasers very often assume that something which is more expensive must be of higher quality - you might even find it tastes better just because you paid more for it . This is why for a long time Kellogs had the slogan " We do n't make cereals for anyone else . "

I m guessing memory and hardware had a lot to do with it . You crossed the checkpoint in super Mario Bros , and died before the end , you would respawn halfway a thru the llevel as a little Mario . A very simple if - then , compared to storing power ups and exact location on the level map . As games got more complex , they could add more nuances and info , like health bar , weapons and ammo as you crossed the checkpoint , but you 'd still start out at the predesignated place , with the bad guys in the same position as they always were . ( I 'm thinking specifucally about Timesplitters 2 on GameCube ) . Compare that to how much info is on a quicksave now , your position , health bar , weapons , pickups , items , exact location on a 3d map , enemies position , health , weapons and exact location . That 's a lot more info to save on a limited flash memory based game where all memory cards are small capacity , external and expensive . I remember a season of madden took up like 30 " blocks " of my 16 block memory card , while Timesplitters took up 1 or 2 , so i had to go buy a larger capacity one to play madden on season mode . Devs used these limitations as an element of the gameplay . In timesplitters , if you wanted to have a any shot beating one of the last levels , you needed to get the checkpoint with damn near full health . Otherwise , you 'd need to start the level over . Resident Evil made you collect ink ribbons and find a typewriter to save your progress , and dying would bring you back to your save previous save point . I do n't know how big these flash memory cards actually were , but at that time i thought a 256 mb memory stick was pretty damn good . Consoles with large hard drives and installed games have opened up more quicksave options with a lot more info , since storage is no longer at a premium .

It is actually ambient sound being amplified by the shell , which is acting as a resonating chamber . You know how horns have flared bells , and drums and stringed instruments have large hollow areas ? Those are resonating chambers that exist for the sound to bounce around in to become louder . Anything that has an enclosed area made of materials that reflect sound waves can produce this effect . The shell works the same way , but the sound that 's resonating is just whatever sounds are in your environment . It 's not air and it 's not blood flowing through your ears , though those are common misconceptions . If you hold a shell or cup to your ear in a sound - proof room with no noise around , you wo n't hear anything . Hiccups are the sudden, spasmodic contractions of the diaphragm muscle, which separates the chest from the abdomen. The diaphragm is a large, dome-shaped muscle that helps you breathe by contracting and relaxing. When the diaphragm contracts suddenly and repeatedly, it can cause a person to hiccup.

There are many different things that can cause hiccups. For example, eating or drinking too quickly, swallowing too much air, or having an irritation in the esophagus (the tube that connects your mouth to your stomach) can all cause hiccups. Stress and excitement can also cause hiccups.

There are a few different techniques that people use to try to stop hiccups. Some people believe that being startled or drinking a glass of water quickly can help stop hiccups. Others believe that holding your breath or swallowing a teaspoon of sugar can help. While these techniques may not work for everyone, they can sometimes help stop hiccups by interrupting the pattern of spasmodic contractions of the diaphragm.

It's not exactly clear why these techniques sometimes work, but it is thought that they may help to reset the signals in the brain and nervous system that control the diaphragm muscle. In any case, hiccups are usually not a serious problem and will go away on their own after a short period of time.

Sure! Smells work by tiny particles called molecules. These molecules are given off by things around us, like flowers or food. When you breathe in, you're also taking in these tiny molecules. They go into your nose and reach special sensors inside your nose that can detect different smells. These sensors send a message to your brain, and your brain tells you what the smell is. It's kind of like a secret code. Each smell has its own special set of molecules that your nose knows how to recognize. When your nose gets a message from one of these sets of molecules, it can figure out what the smell is and tell your brain. So when you smell something, you're not actually inhaling the thing itself. You're inhaling tiny pieces of it that are floating through the air. Does that make sense?

Twitter is a social media platform that allows users to send and read short messages called "tweets." These tweets can be up to 280 characters long and can include text, images, and links to other websites. Users can create a profile on Twitter and follow other users to see their tweets in their timeline. Users can also like, reply, and retweet other users' tweets. Twitter is similar to Facebook in that it is a social media platform that allows people to connect and share information and content. However, Twitter is generally known for its focus on short-form content and real-time conversations, whereas Facebook is known for its focus on longer-form content and connecting with friends and family.

In Australia, power plugs with two prongs are used for appliances that use alternating current (AC) electricity, which is the type of electricity that is used in most homes and buildings. These plugs have two prongs because AC electricity alternates between positive and negative charges, and the two prongs are used to carry these charges to and from the appliance. Power plugs with three prongs, on the other hand, are used for appliances that use direct current (DC) electricity. These plugs have three prongs because DC electricity flows in only one direction, and the three prongs are used to carry the electricity to and from the appliance. The three-pronged plugs also have a third prong called the "earth" or "ground" prong, which is used as a safety feature. If there is a problem with the appliance or the electrical wiring, the ground prong helps to safely direct any excess electricity away from the appliance and into the ground, which can help to prevent accidents or injuries. I hope that helps to explain the difference between two-pronged and three-pronged power plugs! Let me know if you have any other questions.

It is difficult to accurately diagnose a condition without a thorough physical examination and possibly additional testing. However, the symptoms you are describing could potentially be caused by a number of different conditions, including:

Pleurisy: This is an inflammation of the pleura, which is the thin layer of tissue that lines the inside of the chest wall and surrounds the lungs. It can cause chest pain that worsens with deep breathing, coughing, or sneezing.

Pneumonia: This is an infection of the lungs that can cause chest pain, difficulty breathing, and coughing.

Muscular strain or injury: Chest pain that occurs with coughing or deep breathing can sometimes be caused by a strain or injury to the muscles in the chest or upper abdomen.

Gastrointestinal issues: Pain in the upper left abdomen can also be caused by gastrointestinal issues such as acid reflux, inflammatory bowel disease, or a peptic ulcer.

It is important to consult with a medical professional for a proper diagnosis and treatment plan. In the meantime, try to avoid activities that exacerbate the pain and consider taking over-the-counter pain medication as directed.

Microsoft Access is a tool used to create and maintain databases . A mortal can use it by double - clicking the Microsoft Access icon to open the program and performing any function that the program allows . A mortal can use it well by looking online for tutorials , or by taking a class / buying a DIY book . A brief overview as to how it works : In Access , there are four major object types . These objects are Tables , Forms , Queries and Reports . Tables are used to store raw data . They look very similar to that of an Excel spreadsheet , but act a little differently ( i.e. you can not type a formula into a blank cell in a table ) . Multiple tables can be linked together by a common identifier to provide more robust reporting . For example , a business may have a table containing customer information , another containing product information , and another containing sale information . When inputting a record into the Sale table , you can reference a customer identifier and a product identifier which will link the sale to any information found in the record of the respective customer and product tables . Forms are used to enter / display information on / from a table . A form is like a graphical user interface that you can design to improve data entry efficiency . You can also use a form to display information that has already been entered and is useful if you want to print out a hard copy . From the previous example , you can display the record of the sale you entered into an invoice which can be printed and given to the customer . Queries are used to filter information found in tables . You can add any field to a query that is found in the source table or is linked to the source table . For example , you could query a part identifier to determine which customers purchase the part . Another example , you could query a sale date field on the sale table to determine your total sales for that day . Reports are straightforward . They are similar to forms in the sense that they redisplay information from tables . The difference is primarily that a form will only display one record from a table while a report can show all records . You can then perform mathematical operations on certain numbers / values to get a clearer understanding of the information that is presented . For example , you could run a sales report that groups customers together , totals their YTD sales and then shows a grand total at the bottom . Again , that is a very brief overview and if you are interested you should seek out additional resources . It is a very in - depth program capable of performing many functions , but will require training in order to use . I 'm sure you could use it for studying or contributing to a blog , so long as your goal is to obtain and refine raw data . If you 're contributing to a football blog for example , you could track player stats and refine them to identify trends / interesting stats , etc . Hope that helps .

Facial hair in humans is controlled by hormones called androgens, which are present in both men and women. However, men have higher levels of androgens than women, which is why men are more likely to grow facial hair. In our ape ancestors, both males and females likely had facial hair as a way to protect their skin from the sun and to keep warm in cold weather. However, as humans evolved and began to live in different environments, some traits that were once useful became less necessary. For example, as humans began to live in hotter climates, the need for thick body hair to keep warm diminished, and as a result, humans lost much of their body hair. Similarly, as humans began to use clothing to protect themselves from the elements, the need for facial hair to protect the skin from the sun and to keep warm also diminished. As a result, women lost much of their facial hair, while men continued to have more facial hair due to their higher levels of androgens.

When we are tired, our bodies don't function as well as they do when we are well-rested. This can show up in a variety of ways, including dark circles or sunken eyes.

One reason that our eyes might look sunken or dark when we are tired is because our skin becomes less elastic and less moisturized when we don't get enough sleep. As a result, the skin around our eyes can look and feel drier, which can make the area around our eyes appear sunken.

Another reason that our eyes might look sunken or dark when we are tired is because our bodies produce less collagen when we don't get enough sleep. Collagen is a protein that helps to keep our skin firm and plump, and without enough of it, our skin can become thinner and more prone to sagging. This can make the area around our eyes look sunken.

So, in short, when we are tired, our bodies don't work as well as they do when we are well-rested, and this can show up in a variety of ways, including dark circles or sunken eyes.

Public policy is the set of laws, regulations, and actions taken by governments to address public issues. It is created through a process that involves various stages, including the identification of a problem, the formulation of a policy proposal, the decision-making process, and the implementation and evaluation of the policy.

The process of creating public policy often involves the following steps:

Identify the problem: The first step in creating public policy is to identify a problem that needs to be addressed. This may be a social, economic, or environmental issue that affects the general public.

Formulate policy proposals: After the problem has been identified, policy makers will consider different options for addressing the issue. These options are known as policy proposals.

Decision-making process: The decision-making process involves deciding which policy proposal is the most appropriate to address the problem. This process may involve input from various stakeholders, such as government agencies, community groups, and interested individuals.

Implementation: Once a policy has been chosen, it must be implemented. This involves creating a plan for implementing the policy, including allocating resources and setting goals.

Evaluation: The final step in the process of creating public policy is to evaluate the effectiveness of the policy. This may involve collecting data and analyzing the results to determine if the policy has achieved its intended goals. If the policy is not effective, it may need to be modified or replaced with a different approach.

Sure, I can explain why we don't yet have a vaccine for HIV like you're five. HIV, or human immunodeficiency virus, is a virus that attacks the body's immune system and can lead to AIDS (acquired immunodeficiency syndrome). A vaccine is a substance that helps the body build immunity to a disease, so that it can fight the disease more effectively if it is ever exposed to it.

One reason why we don't have a vaccine for HIV yet is that it is a very complex virus. It can mutate (change its shape) easily, which makes it difficult for the body to recognize and fight. This makes it hard to create a vaccine that will work against all the different strains (types) of HIV.

Another reason is that HIV attacks the immune system, which is the part of the body that helps fight off infections. This means that the body may not respond to a vaccine in the same way that it does to other vaccines, which makes it harder to create an effective HIV vaccine.

Scientists and researchers are working hard to find a way to create a vaccine for HIV, but it is a difficult and complex process. It may take many more years of research before we are able to develop a safe and effective vaccine for HIV.

It is important to note that rheumatoid arthritis (RA) and rheumatism are two different conditions. RA is an autoimmune disorder that causes inflammation in the joints and surrounding tissue, while rheumatism is a general term that refers to a variety of conditions that cause pain and swelling in the joints and muscles. Penidure injections are not a treatment for either condition.

The main goal of treatment for RA is to reduce inflammation and prevent joint damage. This can be achieved through a combination of medications, physical therapy, and lifestyle changes. Penicillin is not a commonly used treatment for RA.

There are several different types of medications that are used to treat RA, including nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs), disease-modifying antirheumatic drugs (DMARDs), and biologics. Each of these medications has its own set of potential side effects, and it is important to discuss the risks and benefits with a healthcare provider before starting any treatment.

It is also important to work with a healthcare provider to develop a treatment plan that is tailored to your specific needs and goals. This may involve a combination of medications, physical therapy, and lifestyle changes, such as exercise and stress management techniques.

It is not clear whether there is a cure for RA, but with proper treatment, it is possible to manage the condition and reduce the severity of symptoms. It is important to continue working with a healthcare provider and follow their recommended treatment plan in order to achieve the best possible outcomes.

The universe is indeed expanding, but this expansion is happening on a very large scale. On the scale of our solar system, the distances between the sun and the planets are not increasing significantly due to the expansion of the universe. The reason the planets are able to maintain their orbits around the sun is due to the force of gravity. Gravity is a force that attracts two objects with mass towards each other. The sun has a very large mass, and so it exerts a strong gravitational force on the planets, pulling them towards it. The planets are constantly being pulled towards the sun, but because they are moving in a circular orbit, they never actually reach the sun. Instead, they keep "falling" towards the sun but miss it due to their sideways motion, which keeps them in orbit. So, to summarize, the expansion of the universe is happening on a much larger scale than the solar system, and therefore it does not significantly affect the orbits of the planets around the sun. The planets are able to maintain their orbits because of the force of gravity pulling them towards the sun.

Salt is a preservative in food because it helps to prevent the growth of bacteria. Bacteria need water to grow, and salt helps to remove water from the environment. When salt is added to food, it draws out some of the water and creates an environment that is less hospitable for bacteria to grow. This can help to extend the shelf life of the food.

As for the second part of your question, it is possible for food to become less salty over time. This can happen if the food is stored in a humid environment, as the salt may dissolve in the water and be absorbed by the food. It can also happen if the food is cooked or processed in a way that removes some of the salt. However, in general, the salt content of food does not change significantly over time unless it is actively being removed.

Hydrogen can be produced through a variety of methods, including:

Steam methane reforming: This is the most common method for producing hydrogen. It involves reacting methane (CH4) with steam at high temperatures to produce carbon dioxide (CO2) and hydrogen (H2). The chemical reaction can be represented as follows: CH4 + H2O → CO2 + H2

Electrolysis: This process involves using electricity to split water (H2O) into hydrogen and oxygen. The oxygen is released into the atmosphere and the hydrogen is collected for use. The reaction can be represented as follows: 2H2O + electricity → 2H2 + O2

Partial oxidation: This method involves reacting a hydrocarbon, such as methane, with oxygen to produce hydrogen and carbon monoxide (CO). The reaction can be represented as follows: CH4 + O2 → CO + 2H2

Biomass gasification: This process involves breaking down organic matter, such as wood or agricultural waste, into a gas mixture that contains hydrogen. The gas mixture is then purified to remove impurities and the hydrogen is collected for use.

Fermentation: This method involves using microorganisms, such as bacteria or yeast, to convert organic matter into hydrogen gas. This process is often used to produce hydrogen from renewable sources, such as agricultural waste or plant material.

With renting / buying a house here are some things to consider . Firstly in your life time you have a set finite income ( eg 1 million dollars ) . This is the sum of all your wages / income / investments added up . EVERY TIME you pay interest you are subtracting from that amount . If you pay $ 1000 interest you not only subtract $ 1000 from the million , but all the money you could earn with that $ 1000 ( eg $ 1000 of property might increase to $ 5000 over your life ) . So really $ 1000 interest = $ 6000 lost from your million . So if you can find cheap rent then saving up money and purchasing a house outright or even just a larger chunk of the house can be beneficial ( you pay less interest ) . The amount of interest you save is MASSIVE on large amounts , like a $ 100000 house might cost $ 500000 if you pay it off over a long time . So saving more and waiting can save you money in the long term ( credit sucks bad ) . People always say property increase at X% , so your money is going up X% . Not really because you ALWAYS need somewhere to live . So if you sell your house after ten years all the other houses you now need to buy have gone up X% too . The X% increase is really no advantage unless you somehow get a house that goes up more than the average . Investment . While your property goes up X% there might be investments that go up more than X% so you may be better to rent , save and invest in these other investments . People like houses because , they are generally safe , they understand them , and they need somewhere to live ( kills 2 birds with one stone ; habitation + investment ) . Probably a good investment would be to see a financial adviser to help you get on the track that is best for you . General advice like mine may be inapplicable to your circumstances .

Prohibition, or the ban on the production, sale, and transport of alcohol, was a policy that was supported by a group of people called the temperance movement. The temperance movement believed that alcohol was harmful and caused social problems, such as crime and domestic violence. They also believed that Prohibition would improve people's health and make society better. To gain support for Prohibition, the temperance movement held meetings, gave speeches, and wrote articles to try to convince people to join their cause. They also worked to get politicians to pass laws that would make Prohibition a reality. In the end, they were successful in getting the 18th Amendment to the United States Constitution, which made Prohibition the law of the land. However, Prohibition ultimately proved to be difficult to enforce, and it was later repealed by the 21st Amendment.

Sure! When you watch TV, the images and sounds are created by small electronic devices called transistors. These transistors send tiny electrical signals through wires to your TV's speakers, which then turn the electrical signals into sound waves that you can hear. Even when the volume on your TV is turned off, these electrical signals are still being sent through the wires to the speakers. And because the speakers are still receiving these signals, they can still produce sound waves, even if the volume is turned down low. So, when you hear a TV from another room, it's because the sound waves produced by the speakers are traveling through the air and reaching your ears, just like how you can hear someone talking to you from across the room. As a college student with limited funds, there are several options you can consider to make the most of your money:Look for ways to save money: Consider reducing your expenses by finding ways to save money on things like food, transportation, and housing. For example, you could try to reduce your food costs by cooking at home instead of eating out, or by shopping at discount stores or farmer's markets. This could be a job on campus or in the local community, and could be related to your field of study or something completely different.Seek out scholarships and grants: There are many scholarships and grants available for college students, and these can be a great way to pay for your education without having to take out loans. You can search for scholarships and grants through your school's financial aid office, online databases, and local organizations.Consider alternative financing options: If you need more funds than you have available, you may want to consider alternative financing options, such as a part-time job, a personal loan, or a crowdfunding campaign. Be sure to carefully research and compare the different options available to you to find the one that best meets your needs.Use your resources wisely: As a college student, you may have access to resources such as the library, student center, and career services that can help you save money and find opportunities. Make use of these resources to help you get the most out of your college experience.

There are several options you can consider for raising finance to build a home for your family. Here are a few options you might want to explore:Mortgage: One of the most common ways to finance a home construction project is through a mortgage. A mortgage is a loan that is secured by the property you are building. To qualify for a mortgage, you will need to have a good credit score and a stable income, as well as a down payment of at least 20% of the total cost of the project.Home equity loan: If you already own a home, you may be able to use the equity you have built up in your current home to finance the construction of a new one. A home equity loan is a loan that is secured by the equity in your current home.Personal loan: If you don't own a home or don't have enough equity in your current home, you may be able to take out a personal loan to finance the construction of your new home. Personal loans are unsecured loans that are based on your creditworthiness and income.Construction loan: A construction loan is a short-term loan that is specifically designed to finance the construction of a new home. These loans are typically disbursed in stages, as the construction project progresses.Private financing: If you don't qualify for a mortgage or a personal loan, you may be able to find a private investor who is willing to finance the construction of your new home. This can be a riskier option, as private investors may charge higher interest rates and may require collateral.Government programs: In some cases, you may be able to qualify for government programs that can help you finance the construction of a new home. For example, the U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) offers a program called the Single Family Housing Direct Home Loans program, which provides financing to low- and moderate-income families to build homes in rural areas.Before deciding on a financing option, it's a good idea to shop around and compare the terms and conditions of different loans and financing options to find the one that best meets your needs and budget. It's also a good idea to work with a financial advisor or mortgage broker who can help you understand your options and guide you through the financing process.

If you have a gap in your domestic health insurance coverage, it is possible that your premiums may increase when you try to purchase a new health insurance policy. This is because insurance companies consider a number of factors when determining the premiums for a policy, including your health history and any pre-existing conditions you may have. If you have a gap in your coverage, the insurance company may view you as a higher risk and may charge higher premiums as a result.To minimize the potential for higher premiums, it is important to maintain continuous coverage whenever possible. This can be done by enrolling in a short-term health insurance plan or by finding other ways to maintain coverage, such as through a group plan offered by an employer or through a government program like Medicaid.If you do experience an increase in premiums due to a gap in coverage, there are still ways to lower your premiums. Some options include shopping around for a policy with more favorable terms, increasing your deductible, or taking advantage of any discounts or subsidies that may be available. It may also be helpful to speak with a licensed insurance agent or broker, who can help you understand your options and find a policy that meets your needs and budget.

Sure! A semi-colon is a special punctuation mark that we use in writing to help us connect two sentences together. Imagine that you are telling a story to your friend and you want to add more information or details to your story. You can use a semi-colon to join two sentences together to make it easier for your friend to understand.

For example, let's say you are telling your friend about your day at the park. You might say: "I went to the park today; I saw a lot of ducks swimming in the pond." The semi-colon helps to connect the two sentences together, so it's easier for your friend to understand that you did two things: went to the park and saw ducks.

Another way to use a semi-colon is to separate items in a list. For example, if you are making a list of your favorite toys, you might write: "My favorite toys are: stuffed animals; dolls; and action figures." The semi-colons help to separate the different items in the list and make it easier to read.

So, in short, a semi-colon is like a little "break" in a sentence that helps us connect two sentences or items in a list together. Does that make sense?

It's generally a good idea to have an emergency fund that you can use to cover unexpected expenses or financial setbacks, such as a job loss or medical emergency. How much you should save will depend on your individual financial situation, including your income, expenses, and financial goals.Here are a few things to consider when determining how much to save in your emergency fund:Start by saving enough to cover three to six months' worth of essential expenses, such as housing, food, transportation, and healthcare. This will give you a cushion to fall back on if you experience a financial emergency.If you have a stable job and a steady income, you may be able to save less. If you feel comfortable taking on more risk, you may be able to save less in your emergency fund. However, if you prefer a more conservative approach, you may want to save more.In general, it's important to have an emergency fund that you can tap into if you need to, but it's also important to balance your savings with other financial goals, such as paying off debt, saving for retirement, and building wealth.

The naming scheme in use today was inherited from the JANET Name Registration System , which pre - dates the Internet ( and DNS ) . The Joint Academic Network was divided up primarily into URL_1 ( for academic institutions ) , URL_0 ( for companies ) and UK.MOD ( for the Ministry of Defence ) . When the UK country code top level domain was registered in 1985 ( IIRC ) it made sense to make use of the naming convention that was already established ( although JANET addresses began with the country and become more specific , the opposite of DNS names ) . This is also the reason why use .uk as our country code top level domain . Countries are required to use their ISO-3166 - 1 alpha-2 code which in our case is gb but our pre - existing naming system gave us an exemption from this rule .

Historically , exits were n't always numbered ( they 're not all numbered here in LA ) but while the Interstate Highway System was built , it was encouraged in 1961 and then required in 1970 to number exits . California got a waiver since many exits were already built unnumbered . Some states built mile - based exit numbers while many states built sequential exit numbers but that lead to the situation where several exits were built in between two sequential exits so the exits had be renumbered with a letter appended to the number . IIRC , I saw an exit with a G ( six exits added after numbering ) appended to it in Ohio . This led to states adopting mile - based exit markers starting sometime in the 1970s ( Georgia , for example , switched from sequential to mile - based markers starting in 2000 ) . In 2009 , the Federal Highway Administration made mile - based exit numbering mandatory and each state had to submit a plan to number their exits using mile - based markers . Due to budget issues , a lot of states have n't been able to stick to their plans and have had to extend the time they need to number their exits ( California used to have a plan that would have numbered all exits by 2004 but it has n't been completed yet and there 's no deadline for completion now ) .

A small bowel obstruction is a blockage in the small intestine, which is a part of the digestive tract. The most common causes of small bowel obstruction are:

Adhesions: Scar tissue that forms after surgery or inflammation can cause the small intestine to stick together, resulting in a blockage.

Hernias: Hernias occur when a part of the intestine protrudes through a weakness in the abdominal wall, causing a blockage.

Crohn's disease: This chronic inflammatory bowel disease can cause scarring and narrowing of the small intestine, leading to a blockage.

Intestinal tumors: Tumors in the small intestine can grow large enough to obstruct the intestine.

Foreign bodies: Accidentally swallowing a foreign object, such as a toy or piece of food, can cause a blockage in the small intestine.

Volvulus: This is a condition in which a portion of the intestine twists upon itself, causing a blockage.

Symptoms of a small bowel obstruction may include abdominal pain, nausea, vomiting, constipation, and bloating. If left untreated, a small bowel obstruction can lead to serious complications, including infection and perforation of the intestine. Treatment typically involves surgery to remove the blockage.

Many people are afraid of clowns because they find them unsettling or creepy. There are a few different reasons why people might feel this way. One reason is that clowns often have exaggerated features, such as large noses, bright makeup, and big, fluffy hair. These features can be intimidating or off-putting to some people. Another reason is that clowns are often associated with prankster behavior and mischievousness. This can make them seem unpredictable and potentially dangerous to some people. Finally, clowns are often depicted as being evil or sinister in movies and TV shows. This portrayal can contribute to the fear of clowns, as people may become conditioned to associate clowns with negative feelings. Overall, fear of clowns is a common phobia, but it is not something that everyone experiences. Some people find clowns to be amusing or entertaining, and don't find them scary at all.

If a futures contract seller defaults, it means they are unable to fulfill their obligation to deliver the underlying asset to the buyer at the agreed upon date and price. This can occur if the seller does not have sufficient inventory of the underlying asset to meet the contract, or if they are otherwise unable to deliver the asset as required by the contract.When a seller defaults, the buyer can choose to either accept delivery of the asset at a later date or seek damages from the seller for any losses they incur as a result of the default. The buyer may also choose to close out their position in the contract by entering into an offsetting transaction with another party. This involves buying or selling a futures contract with the same terms as the original contract, but in the opposite direction, effectively canceling out the original contract.The consequences of a seller defaulting can be significant, as it can lead to financial losses for the buyer and damage to the seller's reputation. In some cases, the default may also trigger the termination of the contract and the liquidation of any related positions.

A medical doctor will spend years learning about diseases : their names , how common they are , various ways in which they present , which systems of the body they affect and how , the ways they can interact with other conditions , how they progress , & c. A pharmacist , on the other hand , spend their time learning about drugs : what they are , how they 're made , what they look like , how they work , how they interact with each other , their various other contraindications , & c. Oh , and what they 're used for . These sets of knowledge are complementary . A pharmacist also gets a more holistic view of the patient . If you have allergies , depression , hormone imbalance , and lower back pain , each of those might be treated by a different doctor . Each doctor has only a passing familiarity with what the other doctors are doing , and probably does n't really care much ; you 're there for one thing . You probably only go to one pharmacist , though , so your pharmacist knows all the drugs you 're on . Your pain meds and depression meds have a deadly interaction ? Good thing your pharmacist caught that and got one of them changed before it killed you !

Cortisone injections are a type of medication that is used to reduce inflammation and pain in a specific area of the body. When a person has an injury, such as a muscle or cartilage tear, the body's natural response is to send inflammation to the area to help it heal. This inflammation can cause swelling, redness, and pain. Cortisone injections can help reduce these symptoms by decreasing the amount of inflammation in the area.

To give a cortisone injection, a healthcare provider will numb the area with a local anesthetic and then use a very thin needle to inject the medication into the injured area. The cortisone works by blocking certain chemicals in the body that cause inflammation. This can help reduce swelling, redness, and pain, and can also help the injury heal faster.

Cortisone injections are usually only used for a short period of time, and are not intended to be a long-term solution for managing pain or inflammation. They can be helpful for people who are experiencing severe pain or inflammation that is not responding to other types of treatment. However, cortisone injections are not suitable for everyone, and a healthcare provider will carefully consider whether they are appropriate for a particular patient based on their individual circumstances.

Dreams are created by the brain during the REM (rapid eye movement) stage of sleep. During this time, the brain is very active and can create vivid and sometimes strange or confusing dreams. It's not fully understood why the brain chooses certain events or memories to include in dreams, but it is thought that dreams may help us process and make sense of our daily experiences and emotions. Dreams can also be influenced by things that have happened to us in the past, things that we are currently worried about or thinking about, and even by things that we have seen or heard during the day. So, the events that appear in your dreams may be a mix of real life experiences, memories, and other things that your brain has been exposed to. It's also possible that the events in your dreams may not have any direct connection to your actual life and may be entirely made up by your brain. Ultimately, the content of our dreams is a mystery and is still being studied by researchers.

Net worth is a measure of how much wealth a person has. It is calculated by taking the total value of all the assets they own (such as cash, investments, real estate, and other property) and subtracting any debts they owe (such as loans or mortgages). So, if someone has a net worth of $73 billion, it means that they own assets worth $73 billion and do not have any debts. It does not necessarily mean that they have $73 billion in cash or other liquid assets that they can access right away. It could be that most of their wealth is tied up in things like real estate or stocks, which can take time to sell or convert into cash. For example, Carlos Slim Helu is a Mexican businessman and investor who has a net worth of $73 billion. This means that the total value of all the things he owns is $73 billion. He may have some of this wealth in the form of cash, but he may also have a lot of it invested in other assets such as companies or real estate.

Humans started using money as a way to make trading goods and services easier. Imagine that you have a bunch of apples that you grew in your garden and you want to trade them for some bread. In the past, people used to trade one thing directly for another, which is called bartering. But it was often difficult to find someone who had exactly what you wanted and was willing to trade it for exactly what you had. 
Money was invented to solve this problem. Instead of trading apples directly for bread, you could trade your apples for money, and then use the money to buy bread from someone else. This made trading much easier because now you could buy anything you wanted as long as you had enough money. 
As for living in a money-less society, it's possible that it could work in a small group of people who all have similar needs and are able to produce enough to meet those needs. However, in a larger society with many different types of people and a wide variety of goods and services, it would be much more difficult. Money helps to create a common way for people to exchange value and to store wealth, which can be useful in a complex society.

A Superconductor is a material that allows electrons to flow through with negligible resistance , and we 're not certain that there * is * resistance at all in a few cases . As a result , very little power is lost to the electromagnetic equivalent of friction . Most Superconductive Materials only * function * as Superconductors at near Absolute - Zero temperatures . However , there are several Superconductors that work at * achievable * temperatures . It 's still * very * expensive to cool any known material to the point where they become superconducting , but it 's affordable enough to see use in laboratory environments . A Room Temperature Superconductor would be a world - reshaping discovery , even if it were only used for power lines . Electricity could be generated * anywhere * and transmitted to * anywhere * without a significant loss of energy to Resistance as heat . Power generated by a Solar Plant in Arizona could be sent to New York City , with less loss of energy to resistance . than New York 's current power plants experience . However , we 've never * found * a Room Temperature Superconductor . It 's a Holy Grail of Science .

Taking a photo of the Milky Way requires a few things: 
1. A camera: You'll need a camera that can take long exposures, which means it can keep the shutter open for a longer time to let in more light. This is important because the Milky Way is usually too dim to see with the naked eye, so you need to take a long exposure to capture it. 
2. A tripod: You'll need a tripod to hold your camera steady while you take the photo. This is important because if your camera moves even a little bit while the shutter is open, the photo will come out blurry. 
3. A dark sky: To see the Milky Way, you need to be somewhere with very little light pollution. This means you need to be far away from cities, which have lots of lights that can wash out the stars. 
4. The right time of year: The Milky Way is only visible in the night sky at certain times of the year. It's best to see it in the summer months when it's high in the sky. 
Once you have all of these things, you can set up your camera on the tripod and take a long exposure photo of the Milky Way. The photo will come out looking like a bright band of light across the sky, with lots of stars visible all around it.

Psychological barriers in the context of marketable securities refer to certain price levels that are believed to have psychological significance for market participants and can influence their trading behavior. These price levels can act as resistance or support for the security's price, meaning that the security may have difficulty breaking through or may be more likely to bounce off of these levels.For example, a security that is trading at a price of $100 may be perceived as having a psychological barrier at the $100 level. If the security's price moves above $100, market participants may be more likely to buy it, believing that the security is undervalued and has room to grow. On the other hand, if the security's price falls below $100, market participants may be more likely to sell it, believing that the security is overvalued and may decline further in value.Psychological barriers can be formed based on a variety of factors, such as round numbers, historical price levels, and market sentiment. They are not necessarily based on any fundamental characteristics of the security itself, but rather reflect the collective psychology of market participants.

Rocket thrusters work by using a fuel source and an oxidizer to create a chemical reaction that produces hot gases. These hot gases are then expelled out of the back of the rocket through a nozzle, which is a specially shaped opening that helps to accelerate and direct the flow of gases. The shape of the nozzle determines the shape of the flame that is produced by the rocket.

In the case of a rocket, the nozzle is usually shaped like a cone, with a small opening at the front and a wider opening at the back. This cone-shaped nozzle helps to create a narrow, concentrated jet of hot gases that can be directed in a specific direction. The gases are expelled at a very high speed, which generates a lot of thrust and propels the rocket forward.

The flame of a rocket is different from the flame of a flamethrower, which is designed to produce a wide, diffuse flame. A flamethrower is used to create heat and light, rather than to produce thrust, so it doesn't need a nozzle to focus the gases. Instead, the flamethrower simply allows the gases to escape in all directions, which creates a wide, spreading flame.

Itch follows the same neural tracts as pain ( which are separate from vibratory , fine discrimination , and proprioceptive sensation tracts ) but it is n't pain because pain causes a recoil reflex , whereas itch causes a scratch reflex . Largely , the stimulus to itch is histamine release and detection by neurons , but there are other stimuli . Interestingly , mast cells , which are white blood cells in your skin ( and responsible for a lot of allergy - related stuff ) degranulate and release histamine when stimulated . They can be stimulated by allergens ( like in drug reactions , atopic dermatitis , hives , poison ivy , etc ) or other stuff ( like hot water in the shower ) . Once they release the histamine , the nerves pick it up and you process it as itch .

Hiroshima and Nagasaki are cities in Japan that were subjected to atomic bombings during World War II. Pripyat is a city in Ukraine that was abandoned after the Chernobyl nuclear disaster, and Bikini Atoll is an atoll in the Marshall Islands that was used for nuclear testing by the United States.

The reason that people can still live in Hiroshima and Nagasaki, but not in Pripyat or the Bikini Atoll, is because the levels of radiation in these places are different. The atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki released large amounts of radiation into the environment, but the radiation levels have decreased significantly over time and are now safe for people to live in. In contrast, the Chernobyl disaster released a much larger amount of radiation, and the area around the nuclear power plant is still considered too dangerous for human habitation. The Bikini Atoll was also heavily contaminated by nuclear testing, and some parts of the atoll are still unsafe for human habitation.

So, the main reason that people can still live in Hiroshima and Nagasaki is because the levels of radiation there are much lower than in Pripyat or the Bikini Atoll, and are now considered safe for human habitation.

It is important to be aware that engaging in unprotected anal sex carries a high risk of HIV transmission, as well as other sexually transmitted infections (STIs). HIV is transmitted through the exchange of body fluids, and the lining of the anus is more prone to tearing and exposure to HIV than the lining of the vagina or mouth. This means that the risk of HIV transmission during anal sex is higher than during other sexual activities.

The chances of getting HIV after unprotected anal sex depend on several factors, including the HIV status of the partner, the presence of other STIs, and the presence of any cuts or abrasions on the skin. It is also important to consider the prevalence of HIV in the community where the sexual encounter took place, as HIV is more common in some populations than others.

If you are concerned about the possibility of HIV transmission, it is important to get tested as soon as possible. HIV testing should be done at least 3-6 weeks after potential exposure, as it can take that long for the virus to be detectable in the body. You can also be tested for other STIs at this time. It is important to note that some STIs, including HIV, may not show up on a test until several weeks or even months after infection, so it is important to practice safe sex and get tested regularly to ensure that you are not unknowingly transmitting HIV or other STIs to your partners.

20/20 is n't perfect vision , it 's average vision . What it essentially says is you can see something at 20 feet what the average person can see at 20 feet . If you had , say , 200/20 vision , you would have amazing vision : you could see at 200 feet what the average person could see at 20 . Likewise , if you had 20/200 vision , you 'd have terrible vision , because you could only see something at 20 feet what the average person could see at 200 feet .

It is a coolant . The magnets in and MRI are incredibly powerful , they use superconductors to make such a powerful magnetic field . With regular copper wire , if you coil the wore and run electricity through it , it forms a magnetic field , and around 1 % of the energy is lost to heat . MRIs are so powerful that the small loss to heat would melt the wires . So , they cool the wires with liquid helium , the coils become superconductors , which lose no energy to resistance at all . Liquid helium is very cold , it is -269C or -452F. It takes a lot of helium , compared to a balloon , because a liquid is much more condensed than a gas . Liquid helium is strange stuff . If you pack a hose with sand , a regular fluid like water will flow very slowly through it . Liquid helium has no friction , the sand wo n't slow it down a bit . If you started it flowing through a tube full of sand , bent the tube into a ring shape , and left it alone , it would keep spinning forever .

The U.S. government does not have a patent on Ebola. It is possible that you are confused by the existence of a patent application with the number CA2741523A1, which was filed by the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) in 2009. This patent application described a method for using a particular strain of Ebola virus as a vaccine in order to protect people from getting infected with the virus. It is important to note that a patent application is not the same as a patent. A patent is a legal document that gives the holder the exclusive right to make, use, and sell an invention for a certain period of time. A patent application is a request to the government to grant a patent. In this case, the HHS filed a patent application in order to protect its work on developing a vaccine for Ebola. The strain of Ebola virus described in the patent application is not necessarily the same as the strain of Ebola that is currently being talked about. Ebola is a highly infectious and often deadly disease caused by a virus that is found in certain parts of Africa. There are several different strains of the Ebola virus, and the one that is causing the current outbreak may be different from the strain described in the patent application.

You get to be called " emperor " if you can convince people to call you that , which means that there 's a lot of variations in the monarchies which claimed to be empires . Emperors claim to have superior rank to kings , so there 's some diplomatic issues to be finessed if someone started demanding to be addressed as ' emperor . ' Historically in Europe , the title connoted some kind of claim to the Roman Empire , which limited to the Byzantine Empire in the East , which was a direct successor state , and the Holy Roman Empire in the West , which was proclaimed by the Pope . Accordingly , any Catholic king who tried to call himself ' emperor ' would run into some problems with the Vatican . Eventually , this started to break down . Constantinople fell , and both the Ottoman Empire and the Russian Empire claimed to be the new successor to Rome , with various success . Napoleon had himself coronated as an Emperor , which was a deliberate way to distinguish himself from the prior kings , assert superiority over the kings of Europe ( some of which were his clients ) , and in particular , challenge the Pope 's authority with the dissolution of the Holy Roman Empire . That more or less opened the gates , particularly for new monarchies which were not trying to restore the pre - Napoleonic order of things . New world monarchies claimed imperial titles in Mexico , Haiti , and Brazil . The former Holy Roman Emperors of Austria did not want to relinquish imperial honors , so the Empire of Austria was proclaimed . Germany became an Empire after it was united by Prussia , as a way to ensure parity with Austria , and assert the emperor 's authority over the once - independent kings in his realm , without demoting them . Victoria was proclaimed Empress after that - not Empress of Britain , but Empress of India , something of a gimmick , but one that allowed her to stay on par with her daughter , who married the German emperor and would otherwise have outranked her . Further afield , the title is a translation of a concept , rather than something that can be literally equal . The general sense of it is some kind of super - king , or king - above - kings . " Emperor " usually translated the Chinese term " huangdi , " literally something like " the august godking , " in extremely rough terms . But it was a title claimed for the head of a united China , in contrast to smaller kings , so Emperor fit . " Tenno " in Japan means " heavenly ruler " which connotes a divine connection , rather than a territorial right . In Persia , " Shahnashah " becomes emperor ; literally , it 's " king of kings . "

There can be a variety of reasons why someone might have difficulty falling asleep, even if they feel tired. Some common reasons include: 1. Stress or anxiety: When we're feeling worried or stressed, it can be hard to relax and fall asleep. 2. Noise or light: If there is too much noise or light in the room, it can be difficult to fall asleep. 3. Uncomfortable bed: If your bed is not comfortable or the room is too hot or cold, it can be hard to fall asleep. 4. Eating or drinking too close to bedtime: Eating a large meal or drinking caffeine or alcohol before bedtime can disrupt sleep. 5. Using screens before bed: Looking at screens (such as phones, tablets, or TVs) before bedtime can affect sleep because the blue light they emit can interfere with our natural sleep-wake cycle. To get a good night's rest, it can be helpful to: 1. Try to relax before bedtime by reading a book, taking a warm bath, or practicing deep breathing. 2. Make sure your bedroom is dark, quiet, and comfortable. 3. Avoid screens for at least an hour before bedtime. 4. Avoid eating or drinking caffeine or alcohol close to bedtime. 5. Try to go to bed and wake up at the same time every day, even on the weekends. This can help regulate your body's natural sleep-wake cycle. I hope these suggestions help! If you continue to have trouble sleeping, it might be a good idea to talk to a doctor or other healthcare professional for more advice.

Not really . If you are using 8 bits for each primary colour , then it uses the same memory whatever combination they are in . Even if there is no green for example you are still using 8 bits to represent 0 for the amount of green . However if you do n't need such a wide range of colours you could use fewer bits to represent them . For example some old systems used 3 bits per primary colour . You could still represent mixes of them , but there are fewer shades . If you wanted to go really extreme you could use a single bit per colour . Not sure of any system that did that though . Then you 'd only be able to have 8 different colours , red , green , blue , yellow , magenta , cyan , white and black . So practically speaking , I 'd say no . It takes the same amount of memory whether you have only a single primary colour or a combination of them .
